preview_text:
The model favors Belgium at 65%, while New Zealand's win probability stands at 11%. The underdog faces a steep task; Belgium's forecast is substantially higher, though the presence of a 24% draw probability indicates the match carries meaningful competitive uncertainty rather than a clear mismatch.

The W/D/L spread reflects a decisive lean toward Belgium without an overwhelming consensus around any single outcome. The draw at 24% is the second-most-likely scenario, suggesting that a single-goal contest or stalemate remains plausible. Belgium's 65% win rate sits well above the underdog's 11%, but the aggregate probability mass outside a Belgium win (36%) leaves room for alternative results in a group-stage context.

The model and market are broadly aligned on the fundamental structure. The sportsbook consensus places Belgium at 73%, roughly 8 percentage points higher than the model, while Polymarket runs even more bullish on the favorite at 76%. These gaps are consistent with typical market-model variation, but the dashboard does not adjudicate which source reflects match conditions more accurately.
